name: Sima de Alcorón to Sima de Manuel Mozo – circular from Refugio de la Zapatilla – Parque Natural del Alto Tajo
name_original: Sima de Alcorón a Sima de Manuel Mozo – circular desde Refugio de la Zapatilla – Parque Natural del Alto Tajo
region_intro: Hiking around Villanueva De Alcorón offers diverse landscapes within the Parque Natural del Alto Tajo. The region is characterized by river valleys, deep gorges ("hoz"), and unique geological formations such as sinkholes ("sima"). Trails often traverse forested areas and open terrain, providing varied scenery for hikers. The elevation changes are generally moderate, making many routes accessible while still offering engaging challenges. Best hiking trails around Villanueva De Alcorón The most popular hiking route is Rambla del Avellano – circular desde Arbeteta – Parque Natural del Alto Tajo, a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail that takes around 3 hours to complete. This moderate route explores the natural park's varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hoz Somera and Castillejos – circular from Carrascosa (PR-CU 01), a moderate 4.6 miles (7.4 km) path. This trail features impressive gorges and rock formations. Local hikers also love the Camino de la Escaleruela a Cascada de la Escaleruela – desde Zaorejas – Parque Natural del Alto Tajo, a 8.7 miles (14.0 km) trail leading through natural park scenery to a waterfall, often completed in about 4 hours 45 minutes. Hiking around Villanueva De Alcorón is defined by river valleys, deep gorges, and natural park trails. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Villanueva De Alcorón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 90 hikers have used komoot to explore Villanueva De Alcorón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Villanueva De Alcorón?
A: There are 10 hiking trails available around Villanueva De Alcorón. These include 1 easy, 8 moderate, and 1 difficult route, offering options for various skill levels within the Parque Natural del Alto Tajo.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there is one easy hiking trail available. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the generally moderate elevation changes in the region make many trails accessible. Always check the route details for distance and terrain to ensure it suits your group's abilities.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see along the trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty, characterized by river valleys, deep gorges ('hoz'), and unique geological formations like sinkholes ('sima'). You can explore highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5331775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Peñalén&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5373777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail along the Tajo River&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4442967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sima de Alcorón Cave&lt;/a&gt;. One route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119570149/camino-de-la-escaleruela-a-cascada-de-la-escaleruela-desde-zaorejas-parque-natural-del-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de la Escaleruela a Cascada de la Escaleruela&lt;/a&gt;, leads directly to a waterfall.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120710465/rambla-del-avellano-circular-desde-arbeteta-parque-natural-del-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rambla del Avellano – circular desde Arbeteta&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010265852/hoz-somera-and-castillejos-circular-from-carrascosa-pr-cu-01" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz Somera and Castillejos – circular from Carrascosa&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options that showcase the varied terrain of the natural park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Villanueva De Alcorón?
A: The Parque Natural del Alto Tajo, where Villanueva De Alcorón is located, is generally pleasant for hiking from spring through autumn. Spring offers lush greenery and blooming wildflowers, while autumn provides beautiful fall colors. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winters can be cold, and some higher areas might experience snow, requiring appropriate gear.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails?
A: As Villanueva De Alcorón is situated within the Parque Natural del Alto Tajo, dogs are generally permitted on trails, but it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and respect other hikers. Always check specific park regulations or trail signs for any restrictions that may apply to certain areas.

Q: Are there any trails that feature sinkholes or unique geological formations?
A: Yes, the region is known for its unique geological formations, including sinkholes ('sima'). The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120336464/sima-de-alcoron-to-sima-de-manuel-mozo-circular-from-refugio-de-la-zapatilla-parque-natural-del-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sima de Alcorón to Sima de Manuel Mozo&lt;/a&gt; circular route is specifically designed to explore these fascinating natural features, starting from the Refugio de la Zapatilla.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Villanueva De Alcorón?
A: The hiking routes in Villanueva De Alcorón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 15 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the natural park, and the well-maintained paths that lead through river valleys and forested areas.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads?
A: While specific parking details for each trailhead are not provided, it is common for trails within natural parks in Spain to have designated parking areas, especially at popular starting points or visitor centers. We recommend checking the individual route descriptions on komoot for more precise information on parking availability.

Q: Are there any challenging or difficult routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there is 1 difficult route available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027544487/hundido-de-armallones-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hundido de Armallones Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a longer and more demanding trail, covering over 23 km with significant elevation changes, offering a rewarding experience for those looking for a strenuous hike.

Q: Can I find trails that follow the Tajo River?
A: Yes, the region is characterized by river valleys, and you can find trails that run alongside or offer views of the Tajo River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5373777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail along the Tajo River&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight, and many routes within the Parque Natural del Alto Tajo will feature sections near this important waterway.

Q: Are there any places to stay overnight or shelters along the trails?
A: While most routes are day hikes, the region does offer some facilities. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4040502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vado Salmeron Shelter&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight that could be considered for a break or as part of a longer multi-day adventure, depending on its accessibility and amenities. Always plan your overnight stays in advance, especially within a natural park.
